title: Immunohistochemistry protocol optimized at iMM-JLA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bolhr","text":"The immunohistochemistry (IHC) protocol is used to showcase the location of specific proteins in relation to the arquitecture of the tissue. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"330tp","text":"In this method, we use a primary antibody which binds to a specific antigen in the protein of interest. This antibody has a specific host and can be ready to use or undiluted. The secondary antibody is an anti-host IgG which will bind to the primary antibody 1. It's also conjugated with an Horseradish Peroxidase (HRP).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":259,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fkaif","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3muvh","text":"The HRP will react with the Hydrogen Peroxide in the substract of the DAB solution, oxidizing the DAB, creating a brown deposit on the location of the protein of interest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvhnh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3vpdn","text":"Depending on location of the protein, we can have staining in the nuclei, the cytoplasm or the cellular membrane.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"868q7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhto2","text":"Possible variables are:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b46nj","text":"the concentration of the antibody;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3sgma","text":"type of antigen retrieval;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ou95","text":"the pH of the antigen retrieval solution (for Heat Induced Epitope Retrieval (HIER));","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3lrtk","text":"the time of incubation;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e52ap","text":"the tissue used 2.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":16,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c69o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c0ju0","text":"You can use this protocol for Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded samples (FFPE) and Fixed Frozen samples (Gelatin and OCT).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Measure 20 mL of 20X EnvisionTM Flex Wash Buffer Dako Catalog #Ref. K8007
Make up with distilled water to 1 L of total volume

Perform antigen retrieval using PT Module by Thermo Scientific with a cycle of pre-heat to 65ºC and heat to 95ºC for 20 minutes. This equipment, when optimized, allows you to skip the step of deparaffinization in this section, by putting the slides directly through the antigen retrieval step. This equipment allows two buffers with different pH, in which we use EnVision FLEX Target Retrieval Solution High pH Dako Catalog #K8004 and EnVision FLEX Target Retrieval Solution Low pH Dako Catalog #K8005 , depending on the optimization of the antibody.
Clear in Xylene for 00:10:00
Dehydrate with 96% ethanol for 00:02:00
Put slides in lukewarm running water for 00:05:00
Counterstain with Harris hematoxylin for histology Bio-optica Catalog #05-06004E for 00:00:10 filter Hematoxylin before use
Wash in distilled water for 00:05:00

Incubate with EnvisionTM Dako DAB Dako Catalog #Ref. K3468 for 00:02:00 to 00:05:00 After incubation, pour DAB excess on a paper towel and dispose on type IV waste, with tips and eppendorfs used, avoiding spills at any cost. Clean the incubation chamber and surfaces with Bleach.
Wash in 1X EnvisionTM Flex Wash Buffer Dako Catalog #Ref. K8007 3 times for 00:05:00 each

Incubate with secondary antibody EnVisionTM Flex conjugated w/ HRP Dako Catalog #Ref. K4061 for 00:30:00 Room temperature Be sure to match the secondary antibody to the host of the primary antibody (ex: if using mouse anti-CD3 primary, use anti-mouse secondary)
Wash in 1X EnvisionTM Flex Wash Buffer Dako Catalog #Ref. K8007 3 times for 00:05:00 each

Incubate with Primary antibody in the optimized concentration (if needed, dilute in Antibody Diluent Dako Catalog #Ref. 8006 ) for 01:00:00 at Room temperature Normally, around 100μL of diluted antibody per section, depending on the size of the tissue.
Put Protein Block Serum free Dako Catalog #Ref. X0909 on the tissue
Draw a limit on the edge of the tissue with the hydrophobic PAP Pen
Cover the slide dish and incubate slides in the solution for 00:30:00 Hydrogen Peroxide must be added each time when immersing the slides, to obtain a concentration of 3%.
Put 6 mL of Hydrogen Peroxide Solution Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #7722-84-1 at 4 °C in the 100% EMSURE Methanol Supelco Catalog #1.06009.2500 with the slides
Put slides in 200 mL of 100% EMSURE Methanol Supelco Catalog #1.06009.2500 Extemporaneous solution. 100% methanol can be used up to 3 times.
Wash in 1X EnvisionTM Flex Wash Buffer Dako Catalog #Ref. K8007 3 times for 00:05:00 each
Deparaffinize slides with Xylene for 00:10:00
Wash in 1X EnvisionTM Flex Wash Buffer Dako Catalog #Ref. K8007 3 times for 00:05:00 each
If frozen samples: Remove from storage and let dry. If embedded in OCT, put in distilled water for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . If embedded in gelatin, put in PBS for 00:05:00 at 37 °C .
Change Xylene and deparaffinize for another 00:10:00
Place slides in Ethanol 100% for 00:05:00
Place slides in Ethanol 96% for 00:05:00
Place slides in Ethanol 70% for 00:05:00
To complete the hydration, place slides in distilled Water for 00:05:00
Place in 100% ethanol for 00:02:00

Mount with appropriate mounting medium (i.e. Entellan ® Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #1.00869 ) Let the slides dry in a fume hood until complete polymerization of the mounting medium is achieved
creator: Joana G Antunes
